overview.description: Research group on spoken dialogue systems and natural language generation at Charles University Prague
overview.description_long: The Dialog Systems Group at ÚFAL (Institute of Formal and Applied Linguistics, Charles University) conducts research on spoken dialogue systems, chatbots, and natural language generation (NLG). Established as a formal group in 2019 via the PRIMUS-funded NaMuDDiS project, the group is led by Ondřej Dušek and is currently supported by the ERC Starting Grant NG-NLG (2022–2027). Research themes include statistical and neural dialogue management, task-oriented and multi-domain dialogue, NLG evaluation, and data-efficient model adaptation. The group also maintains the open-source Alex Dialogue Systems Framework.
overview.tagline: Dialogue systems and natural language generation research at Charles University Prague

history.origin_story: Formally established in 2019 via the PRIMUS grant NaMuDDiS (Natural Multi-domain Dialogue Systems, PRIMUS/19/SCI/10), which created a new research group for dialogue systems within ÚFAL, Charles University. Prior dialogue research had been ongoing at ÚFAL since at least 2013 under the Vystadial project.

key_milestones.event: Vystadial project — early statistical spoken dialogue system research at ÚFAL
ÚFAL hosted SIGdial 2015 conference (record 149 attendees), organised by Filip Jurčíček
NaMuDDiS PRIMUS grant (CZK 7.1M) formally establishes the Dialog Systems research group
METOD industry collaboration with Agnostix, co-funded by City of Prague
ERC Starting Grant NG-NLG (Next-Generation Natural Language Generation) awarded to Ondřej Dušek; €1.5M, 2022–2027
CEDMO project (digital media safety) adds new collaborative direction

financials.parent_company: Institute of Formal and Applied Linguistics (ÚFAL), Charles University

grants.name: METOD
grants.provider: City of Prague / industry cooperation with Agnostix

notable_publications: Dušek et al. (2020): Evaluating the State-of-the-Art of End-to-End NLG: The E2E NLG Challenge (Computer Speech and Language)
Hudeček & Dušek (2022): Learning Interpretable Latent Dialogue Actions With Less Supervision (AACL-IJCNLP)
Schmidtová et al. (2024): Automatic Metrics in Natural Language Generation: A survey (INLG 2024)

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Academic research group whose mission is to advance the scientific frontier of dialogue systems and NLG. AGI displacement is not a meaningful risk category; the group actively studies and contributes to LLM-era approaches.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Research lab funded by competitive grants (ERC, PRIMUS) and university support. Not subject to commercial market risk.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Currently backed by the ERC Starting Grant NG-NLG (2022–2027, Grant ID 101039303), one of Europe's most prestigious research funding instruments. Prior PRIMUS/19/SCI/10 grant (CZK 7.1M) demonstrates track record of securing competitive funding.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Well-funded academic research group at a leading Czech university with an active ERC grant running to 2027, strong publication record, and established collaborations with both academia and industry. Primary risk is post-2027 grant renewal, which is typical for all research groups.

metadata.notes: No dedicated CNAIP page found. The 'skupiny dialogových systémů' URL (ufal.mff.cuni.cz/dialog-systems) returns 404; group page is effectively https://ufal.mff.cuni.cz/projects/speech-dialog. Team size estimated from researcher profiles (~5–8 active members including PhD students). Filip Jurčíček is also an ÚFAL dialogue researcher but appears to operate somewhat independently from the Dušek-led group.
